questions.question: Who are you when nobody is watching?
What is the story you tell yourself about your own life?
If someone made a documentary about you, what would the title be?
What part of yourself are you still discovering?
How would the 18-year-old version of you feel about where you are now?
What is one thing about yourself you have finally accepted?
Do you feel like your work reflects who you really are?
What would you do for free because you love it that much?
When did you realize what you were meant to do, or are you still figuring it out?
What is the biggest professional risk you have ever taken?
How do you define success for yourself, not how others define it?
If you could start over in any career, what would you choose?
What has your most important relationship taught you about yourself?
What do you think makes a relationship last?
What is the hardest conversation you have ever had with someone you love?
How do you show love, and how do you need to receive it?
What is something your partner does that you never want them to stop?
How have you grown because of your relationship?
What kind of parent did you want to be, and how close are you to that?
What is the hardest part of raising kids that nobody warned you about?
What moment with your kids made you realize everything was worth it?
What are you trying to teach your kids that your parents did not teach you?
How do you handle the guilt that comes with parenting?
What do you hope your kids say about you when they are adults?
How has your relationship with your body changed over the years?
What does taking care of yourself actually look like in your life?
What health scare or wake-up call changed how you live?
What do you do for yourself that keeps you sane?
How do you deal with getting older physically?
What is one thing you wish you had started doing sooner for your health?
What is your relationship with money — does it stress you out or empower you?
What is the smartest financial decision you have made?
What did your parents teach you about money, intentionally or not?
What does financial security actually mean to you?
What would you do differently with money if you could start over?
How much is enough?
How have your friendships changed since your 20s?
Who is the friend you can call at 2 in the morning?
Have you ever outgrown a friendship? What happened?
What do you need from a friendship at this stage of your life?
Do you find it hard to make new friends as an adult?
What is the longest friendship you have and what makes it work?
What keeps you up at night?
How do you deal with stress when it all piles up?
What is the most stressful period of your life you have survived?
Do you take care of everyone else before yourself?
What would your life look like if you had less stress?
What has stress taught you about your limits?
How has your view of your parents changed now that you are older?
What did your childhood teach you that still affects you today?
Is there something from your childhood you are still healing from?
What do you wish your parents had done differently?
What did your parents get right?
When did you finally understand why your parents did what they did?
What is your biggest regret, and what did it teach you?
Is there someone you need to forgive but have not been able to?
Have you forgiven yourself for the mistakes you have made?
What would you say to someone you hurt if you had the chance?
Do you believe in second chances?
What is something you have let go of that used to weigh you down?
Who is someone you have lost that still affects you?
How do you carry the people you have lost with you?
What did grief teach you about love?
Is there something you wish you had said before someone passed?
How has loss changed your perspective on life?
What is a memory of someone you lost that still makes you smile?
Do you feel like the same person you were 10 years ago?
What roles do you play — parent, spouse, worker — and which one feels most like you?
Have you ever had an identity crisis? What triggered it?
What did you give up about yourself to become who you are now?
Is there a part of yourself you miss?
When do you feel most like yourself?
What gives your life meaning?
Has your faith or belief system changed over the years?
What do you turn to when life does not make sense?
Do you believe things happen for a reason?
What is something you believe deeply that you cannot prove?
How do you find hope when everything feels dark?
Where do you feel like you belong?
What community or group has shaped who you are?
How do you give back to the people around you?
What does home mean to you at this point in your life?
Have you ever felt like an outsider? How did you handle it?
What would your ideal community look like?
What is the hardest season your relationship has been through?
How do you keep a relationship strong when life gets in the way?
What do you argue about most, and is it really about that or something deeper?
When did you feel the most connected to your partner?
What compromise has been the hardest for you?
What would you tell someone about to get married?
Do you feel like you have a good balance between work and life?
What do you sacrifice for your career that you wish you did not have to?
How do you switch off from work mode?
What does a perfect day look like for you?
Are you living to work or working to live?
What would you change about how you spend your time?
What do you want to leave behind for the people you love?
What values are you passing on to the next generation?
How do you want to be remembered?
What is the most important thing you are building right now?
If your grandchildren read your story, what would you want them to know?
What is one lesson from your life that you want to make sure is not forgotten?
What was the moment that changed everything for you?
When did you take a different path than everyone expected?
What decision are you most grateful you made?
Was there a crossroads where you almost chose differently? What would have happened?
What event divided your life into before and after?
Looking back, was that turning point luck, hard work, or something else?
What is the hardest thing you have ever been through?
What did your darkest moment teach you about your strength?
Who showed up for you when you needed it most?
What is something hard you are going through right now?
How do you let people in when you are struggling?
What does strength actually look like — is it holding it together or letting it out?
What dream did you let go of, and do you still think about it?
Is it too late to chase something you always wanted?
What would you attempt if you knew you could not fail?
Have your dreams changed, or have you changed?
What is something you still want to accomplish?
What keeps you from going after what you really want?
What are you most grateful for in your life right now?
When was the last time you felt truly content?
What is something small that brings you joy every day?
Do you appreciate what you have or are you always chasing more?
What would you miss most if it was suddenly gone?
What does enough look like for you?
Have you ever mentored someone? What was that like?
What is the best piece of wisdom you can pass along?
Who needs to hear your story?
What mistake do you see younger people making that you wish you could warn them about?
How do you help someone without taking over their journey?
What is something you know now that could save someone else years of struggling?
What do you do purely for the joy of it?
When was the last time you lost track of time doing something you love?
Is there a hobby you dropped that you want to pick back up?
What new skill or hobby have you taken up recently?
How do you make time for yourself when everyone else needs you?
What would you do more of if you had more time?
How do you feel about getting older?
What gets better with age that nobody tells you about?
What is something you worried about at 20 that seems silly now?
Do you feel your age or do you feel like a different age inside?
What do you want the second half of your life to look like?
If you could freeze time at any age, which age would you choose?
What does your home mean to you?
What is your favorite room and why?
How have you made your home reflect who you are?
What does comfort look like at the end of a long day?
Where do you feel safest in the world?
If you could live anywhere, where would it be?
What is the hardest truth about adult life?
What do you pretend is fine that is not?
When was the last time you were truly honest with yourself?
What is something you are afraid to admit out loud?
What keeps you going on the days you want to give up?
What is the bravest thing you do on a regular basis?
How do you show the people you love that you care?
What is the most loving thing anyone has ever done for you?
Do you find it easier to give love or receive it?
What does love look like in your daily life, not the big moments, the small ones?
How has your understanding of love evolved over time?
What do the people closest to you need from you that you sometimes forget to give?
What is the best trip you have ever taken?
Where in the world do you still dream of going?
What did traveling teach you that staying home could not?
What is the most spontaneous thing you have ever done?
Do you crave adventure or stability more at this point in life?
What trip changed the way you see the world?
What does a typical day in your life look like?
What is a small daily ritual that grounds you?
How do you start your morning, and does it set the tone for the day?
What do you do at the end of the day to wind down?
What part of your routine would you never give up?
If you could add one thing to every single day, what would it be?
What season of life are you in right now?
What season was the hardest, and what season was the sweetest?
Are you holding on to a season that has already passed?
What season of life are you most looking forward to?
How do you navigate the transition between life stages?
If your life were a book, what chapter are you in right now?
Are you a creative person? How does that show up in your life?
What is something you have built, made, or created that you are proud of?
When was the last time you made something with your hands?
Do you think creativity is something you are born with or something you practice?
What would you create if time and money were not factors?
How do you solve problems — logic, instinct, or creativity?
What boundary have you set that changed your life?
Do you find it hard to say no? Why?
What is a boundary you wish you had set sooner?
How do you protect your energy from people who drain it?
What is the difference between being selfish and taking care of yourself?
Who in your life respects your boundaries, and who does not?
What memory from your 20s still makes you smile?
Is there a song that takes you straight back to a specific moment?
What do you miss about the way life used to be?
What era of your life would you relive if you could?
What is a tradition from your past that you have carried into the present?
What smell or taste instantly takes you back to a moment in time?
What is the most painful lesson you have had to learn?
What did hitting rock bottom teach you?
What is something you wish someone had told you before you went through it?
How do you handle disappointment?
What made you tougher than you thought you were?
Is there a lesson you keep having to relearn?
When was the last time you had real, unfiltered fun?
Do you still know how to play, or have you forgotten?
What makes you laugh until your stomach hurts?
What is something ridiculous you have done as an adult that felt great?
Who brings out the most fun version of you?
What would your inner kid want you to do more of?
What do you know for sure?
What advice would you give to someone 10 years younger than you?
What is the most important thing life has taught you?
What is something true that most people do not want to hear?
What would you tell your kids about life that school will never teach them?
If you could sum up everything you have learned in one sentence, what would it be?
What are you most afraid of in life?
What fear have you overcome that once felt impossible?
How does fear hold you back, and how does it protect you?
What would you do if fear was not a factor?
What is a fear your parents had that you refuse to pass on?
How do you face something that terrifies you?
What milestone are you most proud of reaching?
What milestone came earlier or later than you expected?
Do you measure your life by milestones or by moments?
What is the next milestone you are working toward?
What milestone did not feel like a big deal at the time but now means everything?
What is a milestone nobody celebrated that you wish they had?
What does the rest of your life look like in your mind?
What are you building toward?
What do you want the next decade to be about?
Are you excited or anxious about the future?
What promise have you made to yourself about how you will live going forward?
If you could guarantee one thing about your future, what would it be?
Is there something you started but never finished that still nags at you?
What conversation do you need to have that you have been putting off?
What relationship needs repair?
What goal have you set aside that deserves another shot?
What would it feel like to finally finish something you have been carrying?
What do you need to let go of to move forward?
What is a simple pleasure that never gets old?
What is the best part of an ordinary day?
What makes you feel alive in a quiet way?
What is something free that brings you more happiness than anything money can buy?
What ordinary moment do you want to remember forever?
What would your perfect lazy Sunday look like?
If your house was on fire and everyone was safe, what one thing would you grab?
What matters more to you now than it did five years ago?
What used to matter to you that does not anymore?
Who are the five most important people in your life right now?
What do you want more of in your life?
If today was your last day, would you be at peace with how you spent it?
What do you know about your family history?
What traits did you inherit from your parents or grandparents?
Is there a family story that gets told at every gathering?
What do you want the next generation to know about where they came from?
What is a piece of your family history you wish you knew more about?
How does knowing where you come from help you know where you are going?
How do you take care of your mental health?
What is something you have worked on emotionally that has made you better?
Do you ask for help when you need it, or do you try to handle everything alone?
What is the most important thing you have learned about your own mind?
How do you know when you are not okay?
What would you say to someone who is struggling but afraid to ask for help?
What is the most courageous thing you have ever done?
When did you stand up for something even though it cost you?
What does courage look like in everyday life?
Have you ever walked away from something that was no longer right for you?
What gave you the strength to make the hardest decision of your life?
What is something you are doing right now that takes quiet courage?
How has your relationship with your siblings changed as adults?
What do you appreciate about your siblings now that you did not as a kid?
Is there a sibling dynamic that still plays out even though you are all grown up?
What is your favorite memory with a brother or sister?
How do you navigate family disagreements as adults?
What role do you play in your family — peacemaker, leader, or something else?
How are you a different person than you were five years ago?
What habit or pattern did you break that changed your life?
What book, podcast, or conversation shifted your thinking?
What are you currently working on about yourself?
Do you think personal growth ever stops, or is it lifelong?
What is the version of yourself that you are becoming?
What meal brings your family together?
What food takes you right back to your childhood?
Do you cook? What is your signature dish?
What is the most memorable meal you have ever had?
What recipe do you want to make sure gets passed down?
What does sitting around a table with the people you love mean to you?
What season speaks to your soul?
Where in nature do you feel the most at peace?
How does the weather affect your mood?
What is the most beautiful natural thing you have ever witnessed?
Do you need nature to reset, or are you more of a city person?
What outdoor memory do you treasure the most?
If your life was a movie, what would the title be?
What has been the main theme of your life so far?
Who are the main characters in your story?
What chapter are you in right now, and what comes next?
What do you want the ending to look like?
Say something right now that you want preserved forever.
